Docs still advertise removed commands in root README, packages/cli/README.md, docs/initialization.md, docs/getting-started.md, docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md, docs/dashboard-tour.md, docs/contributing.md, docs/CONVENTIONS.md. column18: {"id":"E3","status":"completed","findings":"Init state is not target-aligned: packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts still includes humanLockPath/action/content in plan/result, reads templates/fabric/human-lock.json, writes .fabric/human-lock.json, and prints it in plan summaries; initial meta only tracks bootstrap. Follow-up assets are coupled to legacy state: packages/cli/templates/claude-skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md writes rules under .fabric/agents and updates agents.meta; Codex skill preserves human-lock token. sync-meta is the existing deterministic builder for agents.meta + rule-test.index, but scans .fabric/agents/**/*.md and accepts legacy AGENTS.md bootstrap, so doctor --fix needs a replacement rules-based builder over .fabric/rules. Server doctor still runs inspectHumanLock/createProtectedPathsCheck and migrateLegacyLedger, plus read-ledger/audit-log retain legacy .intent-ledger/audit compatibility. Positive replacement API: event-ledger.ts and schemas/event-ledger.ts already write/read .fabric/events.jsonl with typed baseline/rule events; rule-sections/get-rules already support node.content_ref -> .fabric/rules. column18: {"id":"E4","status":"completed","findings":"MCP server surface is centralized in packages/server/src/index.ts: createFabricServer still imports/registers fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry, and index.test currently asserts their deprecated descriptions.
